summary: McKenzie documents his experiment with exact match domain names as an SEO strategy, purchasing christmasbingocards.com for $149 to capture seasonal search traffic for Christmas bingo cards. He outlines a seven-step process for creating a focused mini-site designed to rank quickly for specific keywords, from domain purchase through WordPress setup to content creation. The strategy prioritizes link acquisition over direct sales, reasoning that an attractive, purpose-built free resource will naturally attract links from parents while converting teachers into paying customers. He frames this as a test of Aaron Wall's teachings about exact match domains outranking older, more established sites.
key_points: Exact match domain names can help new sites rank quickly in Google, even past older sites with more links
The mini-site strategy separates link acquisition (free resource) from monetization (commercial product), since people link more readily to non-commercial sites
Seasonal traffic patterns represent massive business opportunities — holidays account for nearly a quarter of his downloads, with Halloween driving his best month every year
The total investment ($157 for domain) compares favorably to ongoing AdWords spend that evaporates weekly, while the domain produces value for years
If the single mini-site experiment works, the strategy scales to multiple seasonal or niche-specific sites
money_quotes: I'd make a mostly non-commercial mini-site focused like a freaking laser on giving people exactly what they wanted, and making it as easy to link to as humanly possible.
It is fairly difficult (though by no means impossible) to get people to link directly to a commercial site.
I'll spend more on AdWords this week alone than the domain cost me, and as of next week its like that money was never spent… but the domain stays up for years.
You're creating a site to be the One Canonical Source On The Internet for your little subniche — it should look the part.
An attractive site makes it very easy for it to collect links from your customers saying 'Hey, Ethyl, I just saw the best site on the Internet for Christmas bingo cards.'
Look at the design! Look at the domain name! Look at the Zen-like purity of purpose!
If it works… why stop at one mini-site?
key_insight: A purpose-built mini-site on an exact match domain, designed to be the definitive free resource for a narrow niche, can acquire links and search rankings far more effectively than trying to rank a commercial site directly.
